desc:
To the south, much of the forest has been cleared away. Perhaps there was once
some sort of habitation there. To the west, the land rises to a low range of
hills, although the road seems to stay somewhat level as it takes advantage of a
notch cutting through the hills. The forests are not as prominent to the north,
and often the hills have bald tops.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The ground gains altitude to the north, where a large plateau dominates the
horizon completely. Here the hills are lower, their sides clad in grass, moss
and young conifers. The eastern slopes of this ridge are too severely steep to
allow and travel in that direction. South of here the Old East Road makes a fast
descent into a bowl-like valley, the slope being sheer and impossible to climb.
name: Bald Hills

desc:
The road passes through a bowl-shaped depression. The drainage seems to be poor,
since the ground at the bottom is quite muddy. Along the northern edge of the
road, the imprints of several huge and booted feet are obvious, even to those
without a modicum of skill in tracking. The tracks head west, up the road, but
disappear soon after leaving the soft mud.
name: Dip in the Road

desc:
A small area of flat land extends from here to the north; to both the east and
west the hills present a challenge to travelers. To the west, some of the slopes
are so severe as to make progress impossible. South of this area, the Old East
Road takes advantage of a break in the terrain to provide a means of moving
westward.
name: By the Road

desc:
The road here is often cut with small gullies caused by water draining from the
hills to the north and west. To the east, the road slopes down into a shallow
valley from whence comes the sounds of a small stream. Further west, the hills
become more severe, with bald crowns sticking high above the dark forests.
name: Old East Road

desc:
At some point in the recent past, many of the trees in this area were chopped
down --- the remains of rotting stumps are scattered throughout. In the centre
of the clearing is a bare patch of earth, with pieces of charred wood protruding
from the ground in several places. Perhaps some foolhardy person decided to
settle here, but whatever structure once stood has long since been destroyed.
name: Small Clearing

desc:
The land falls somewhat to the east - the forest there becomes thinner, but the
undergrowth seems much more prominent. To the north winds the ribbon of the Old
East Road. The road is clearly visible because it runs through a shallow valley.
To the east, the trees give way almost completely in an abrupt transition.
name: Forest

desc:
The undergrowth has almost choked out the forest here --- only a few ancient
gnarled trees have survived amongst the sea of brambles and thorn- bushes that
have taken root here. The ground is fairly low and damp, and seems to slope
slightly to the east. To the west the forest again prevails, and the ground is
much drier.
name: Thick Brambles

desc:
Numerous rivulets feed the growing creek as it meanders southwards. Gravel
glistens from the clear creekbed, shining golds, browns, and reds. Gnarled
tree-roots protrude from the streambank; the water is shaded by the flanking
copses of beech and willow. To the west is a chaotic tumbling of brush and
brambles.
name: A Gravel-bedded Creek

desc:
A large meadow opens up amid the surrounding brush and heath. The road meanders
along its merry way to the north, while south you note a number of dilapidated
structures that appear to be uninhabited.
name: A Wide Clearing

desc:
The Road descends into a valley and crosses a small creek. While the Road has
been eroded somewhat, the colourful streambed is easily forded. There is a
glimmering to the north where the creek seems to originate, to the south the
valley deepens and widens as the creek courses forward.
name: Fordable Creek

desc:
The Road ascends from the valley to the west, curving alongside a large vale. A
large field lies to the south, grassy with some large trees scattered about. A
few tumbled and broken structures lie beyond the field, while in the other
direction woods grow near unto the road, and tall ridges rise above the
treeline.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Here the Road clings close to the feet of the hills, rolling and winding its way
among woods and heather-covered slopes. The land is somewhat pleasant, but there
is an oppressive look to the uplands to the north--somehow an air of evil
radiates from the frowning crags and peaks.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Continuing ever onward, the Road runs straight and true momentarily before
resuming the winding and rolling that renders it difficult to gauge the path
ahead for any great distance. Stained and broken by weather and neglect, the
Road nevertheless bears evidence of recent travel by horse, cart, and the
padding paws of some unknown quadruped.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Fractured and faded by time and neglect, this ancient road still provides a
solid footing as it winds from west to north. Scattered trees line the roadside,
although to the east there are only twisted boles bereft of vitality.
name: The Old East Road

desc:
Here the Road winds through an area of denser forest--the trees nestle closely
looming above you, their branches extended attemping to grasp their brethren
opposite the road. Illumination filters through their limbs, dappling the way
with alternating spatterings of light and shadow. One may continue along the
road to the east or south, or dare to enter the unknown forests bordering the
wide path.
name: The Old East Road

desc:
The Road proceeds through a dark, ominous section of the lonelands; all about
are the withered apparently fire-blasted stumps and skeletal boles of trees. A
trail blasted into the landscape will allow an easy journey north where the land
seems if anything more tortured and inimical to life. Perhaps though, one's
return might not prove so simple...maybe it would be wise to leave this area
hastily.
name: Crossing in the Road

desc:
The Old Road proceeds doggedly onward through the barren surroundings, running
momentarily straight and true. A faint urge to hurry onward tingles in the back
of your mind; the area above the road has an unwholesome cast, while egress to
the south is thwarted by a natural hedge of hawthorn.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Erupting from the hillside, a large spring burbles as it feeds a small creek
that winds along a valley to the south, bisecting the large road there. The grey
hillsides are covered with patches of heather and bracken, pale grasses also
cling along the hills. The surroundings seem unnaturally quiet.
name: Source of the Creek

desc:
Rimmed with ivy-covered walls of large stakes implanted in the ground, this
enclosure is further shrouded by thick accumulations of bushes and brambles that
render this area difficult to discern from the roadway. Large wooden stakes have
been implanted in the ground to allow individuals to tether their mounts.
name: A Concealed Enclosure

desc:
These fields are devoted to the production of fodder for the many beasts of
burden that frequent the caravan station. Bountiful rows of grains and fields of
hay appear to be well tended. In one corner are a few rows of melilot and a
small herb garden. The wooden fence, carpeted with ivy, surrounds and
camouflages the area.
name: Heavily Cultivated Fields

desc:
This meadow is pleasant and well-favoured, possessed of lush grasses and
colourful blooms, complimented by a few scatterings of birch, aspen, and spruce.
The road meanders along its long journey to your north; beyond it the land rises
into jagged uplands. To the east the woods thicken somewhat.
name: A Lightly Wooded Meadow

desc:
This large room seems forsaken, but is less ruined than the exterior of the
building would indicate. Debris is piled in the corners and cobwebs line the
corners, but the walls are built of sturdy oak panels that seem to postdate the
rest of the edifice. Perhaps some intrepid soul decided to refurbish the
structure, but reconsidered staying in this lonesome wilderness.
name: A Musty Old Room

desc:
This hallway seems abandoned and disused, but the walls and rafters still seem
sound. The floor is wooden and covered with a thin layer of dust while the walls
are lined with cobwebs.
name: A Dusty Hallway

desc:
A smaller room adjoining the main chamber has a confined, albeit cozy feel to
it. Fine weavings emblazoned with bright colours line the walls, and some fine
clothing hangs from wooden racks. Bundles and boxes of basic supplies are piled
haphazardly in one corner. An open archway leads back into the common room, and
there is a small wooden door to the south.
name: An Antechamber

desc:
You find yourself in a tavern of the most comfortable and inviting sort,
brimming with the odours of tasty food and frosty ale. Someone has arrayed long
trestle tables with accompanying high-backed chairs of darkened and well oiled
hardwood. Above all sparkles a crystalline chandelier of coloured glass imbuing
the surroundings with a soft relaxing ambience; evidently it is the product of
some advanced dwarven handicraft. Lining the walls are oaken panels limned with
the visages of patrons former and recent -- they are remarkably lifelike,
infused with personality by some remarkably talented artisan.
name: At a Caravanserai

desc:
The room has a spartan quality, but yet retains all the integral trappings to
make it a comfortable and cozy room of respite. A short bed with an oaken
bedstand occupies one corner, with a matching nightstand close at hand. Woven
rugs cover the floor, dyed bright and cheery colours. Hung on the wall are
mementoes and a couple of oil paintings.
name: A Cozy Bedroom

desc:
This room is small in size, but what space there is has been crammed with
supplies and sundries. Barrels, sacks, crates, bundles, casks, tools, spare
weapons, and a plethora of other individual items have been stacked and arranged
neatly to maximise the capacity of the area.
name: A Storage Cellar

desc:
New growth proliferates here, in a process of renewal not yet manifest in the
scarred lands to your north. Young trees roughly uniform in size and age spread
bright green needles towards the sky, and the ground is carpeted with fresh
grasses and prolific wildflowers. A fresh crisp fragance whispers effervescently
on the breeze, and small mammals and birds are seen occasionly gathering
sustenance or merely enjoying the wonders of their fleeting existence.
name: Young Forest

desc:
Perhaps this area was once lush and prolific, but at some point a dire force has
shriveled and browned the entire landscape. Some twisted tree-boles and patches
of dry brush and bramble dot the area, but otherwise it is completely barren.
Rough hills rise behind the road to the north, whereas to the south the forest
is green and growing, in sharp contrast to your current environ.
name: A Withered Landscape

desc:
The slow process of regrowth has begun in earnest here; a verdant carpet of wild
grasses and flowers ripples underneath the fresh arboreal scions sending their
new limbs questing towards the sun. The trampling of small trails in the grasses
dotted with fresh spoor, and small nests of twigs and branches all attest to the
existence of prospering fauna.
name: An Area of Regrowth

desc:
Rising above the surrounding environs, this lush and verdant plateau is well
endowed with oaks and spindling pines. Underneath the trees flourish fields of
strawberries and mountain lingons, their runners writhing and intermingling in a
flurry of red and green. To the distant north, purple mountains rise contrasting
the sky, the view opposite reveals a valley bespoken of healing and wonder.
name: A Forested Plateau

desc:
Thickly cluttered among themselves, whispering amidst the breeze, tall pines and
spruce lie in secret conclave; their thoughts beyond the reckoning of even the
elves. Cloaked in fiery green, the hillsides roll and dip with grassy
dew-spotted rainbows glimmering in the light beneath the trees, descending to
the north into a small rocky ravine that cleaves the highlands denying progress
in that direction. To the east the pines grow sparse, southwards the land rises
into a mighty plateau.
name: Woods near a Ravine

desc:
Nestled in the midst of deep heather and bilberry brushwood is a patch of thick
growing hazels. The prickly blanket of undergrowth effectively camouflages the
area from the nearby road, nevertheless it is possible to observe any nearby
travellers through the bushes.
name: A Patch of Thick Growing Hazels

desc:
The land rises, slowly curving above the landscape, offering a good view of the
surrounding environs. The forest is apparently in the process of rebirth to your
northwest; evident are the low bright green tops of new trees. The land slides
down into a valley east of you--the area looks rather peaceful and inviting for
a fact. Closer upon you lie tall pines and a rich conglomeration of brush and
heather.
name: Low Rise in the Forest

desc:
From each side of the deep valley a steep hillside clothed in thick pine woods
rises from the bottom of the valley to the feet of twin escarpments. It is a
large table of rock furrowed here and there from clefts that the wind and rain
filled with dust, sand, and gravel. The hardy plants of the hills follow the
lines of the furrows, forming miniature hedges, and in the larger crevasses
cades and junipers, mixed with some twisted pines, have formed little dark green
thickets.
name: A Pleasant Valley

desc:
A long, wide terrace of rock stretches between two ravines, covered with small
trees and herbs. Shielded from prevailing winds by a ledge of blue rock, it
overlooks the bottom of a verdant valley above a sheer drop of fifty yards. On
the hillside, slightly apart from the other trees, stands an ancient mountain
ash, whose roots doubtless plunge into some invisible crevasse. Multilated by
lightning, bristling with stumps like a parrot's comb, it stretches its long,
leafy branches over a flat rock, smooth as a mirror and just perfect for a short
respite.
name: A Humped Terrace

desc:
The woodlands prosper amid rolling stone hills and valleys. Rich, verdant
undergrowth of rough yellowish grass, broom, and dogwood flourishes beneath a
canopy of young pines and groups of turpentine trees. Here and there are plum
trees prickly with black dead branches and a few old fig trees suffocated by
their own suckers. A wall of large bare stones seems to be embedded in the base
of a high rock face. A modest wooden door, flanked by small windows has been
constructed in the bluish limestone wall. Beside it is a retaining wall brimming
with rich soil that may at one time have housed a garden.
name: A Vertical Escarpment

desc:
A peaceful shady grove of tall thin pines grow here, not as robust as others
nearby, but nonetheless gleaning a fragile beauty from the rocky earth. The
trees' lithe appeal is accentuated by clusters of clematis, and a few wild
carnations emerge from small fissures with a splash of colour.
name: A Grove of Spindly Pines

desc:
A small game trail dodges between clusters of elm and oak trees, together with a
few grand old hazels. The earth is soft and moist, laden with rich humus, and
supports a preponderance of broom and heather, mingled with vibrantly coloured
wildflowers--clematis, honeysuckle, and larkspur. Fresh animal tracks attest to
the constant use of this small pathway.
name: Path between the Trees

desc:
A preponderance of animal tracks cut between the trees, forging a path among the
otherwise dense undergrowth. Tall and ancient oaks and elms grow proudly all
about you, sheltering the area with their mighty limbs. And beneath their
benevolent shelter all manner of shrubs and bushes thrive, competing with small
wildflowers to cover the ground wherever the wildlife has not trodden all
vegetation into the dirt.
name: A Turning Trail through the Trees

desc:
Although this stretch of the woodland stands close by the road, the thick
undergrowth forms an impenetrable hedge, which defeats any attempt to gain the
thoroughfare. Great oaks lift their lofty heads above you, joining with the
tangled brambles and gorse to shelter and protect this area, which must offer a
home to innumerable small creatures. And, judging by the way the foliage has
been cropped, these woods are also home to some larger game.
name: Deep in the Woods

desc:
Thick brambles and spiky gorse grow in a tangle beneath the tall trees. Great
and ancient oaks and elms stand proudly all around you, their strong boughs
forming a canopy overhead, and their lower limbs joining into the twisted snarl
of undergrowth. To the north this foliage forms a natural hedge, which defeats
any attempt at travel, but to the south and east the forest opens out somewhat.
Progress westward is possible, but the forest appears to be no less forbidding
in that direction.
name: Tangled Woods

desc:
Tall trees and thick undergrowth run rampant just to the south of the road.
Ancient oaks, elms and birch trees stand shoulder to shoulder with younger pines
and firs. And all grows amid a proliferation of bushes and shrubs, the whole
threatening to overrun the thoroughfare. Westward the undergrowth grows yet
thicker, with thick brambles and gorse becoming predominant.
name: In the Forest along the Road

desc:
A wild and healthy expanse of woodland stands sandwiched between the road and
the old lands of a smallholding. Dry stone walls mark the border of the latter.
Built to last, they are nevertheless beginning to submit to the test of time.
Without repair, the constant pressure of the forest is beginning to breach the
barrier. Even the road is not immune, for many trees have grown tall at its very
edge, their branches and the dense undergrowth threatening to encroach upon the
thoroughfare more with every passing day.
name: In the Forest along the Road

desc:
The land here appears to have been denuded of trees long ago, perhaps to serve
as a pasture or field for crops. But whatever purpose it once served, that is
now in the past, and the area is now a fallow sea of weeds and scattered
brushwood. Despite the stone wall, which is still largely intact, the forest to
the west seems bent to reclaim what was once its own.
name: A Long Abandoned Pasture

desc:
The last remnants of a few rotten boards are scattered about the ground. A line
of half-rotted posts stick out of the ground, showing where a fence must once
have stood. These pales describe three sides of a rectangle, the fourth being
comprised of an old stone wall, which stands at the forest border. To one end of
the rectangular enclosure stands a rusted trough, its underside now pierced by
rough holes. Below the trough the ground is marked by the russet stains of the
iron-tainted water which has long run through the breaches in the bottom of the
container.
name: By an Old Sheep Pen

desc:
Thick heather and low-growing bushes are interspersed among the coarse, thin
grasses which cover the gently rolling landscape. Though the ground shows signs
that this was perhaps once a cultivated area, the land here has long since
reverted to nature. Dreary and exposed to the elements, it has a chilly aspect.
But it is in this wild and unkempt mien that its character originates.
name: On a Gently Rolling Heath

desc:
An old stone wall once marked the boundary of the forest here. But it has now
fallen to the tests of time, and to the constant pressure of the encroaching
trees and undergrowth. At the edge of the woods new saplings are springing up,
and though many are cropped and killed by the local wildlife, others thrive. So
it is that the forest spreads once more into lands once taken from it by the
diligent hand of hard-working crofters.
name: At the Edge of the Woods

desc:
The thick woodland opens into a wide glade where the trees stand further apart.
Plant life flourishes here under the lighter canopy. The undergrowth of dense
brambles and broom which predominate in these woods is supplemented by a thick
carpet of lush, green grass and heather. Although the vegetation shows signs of
frequent grazing, it remains in sound health.
name: A Large Glade

desc:
The game trail seems to falter here, splitting to take alternate tracks. To the
west it marches decsisively through the trees, but east and south alike show
signs of some use, both recent and regular. It is as if the creatures which have
forged these paths have alternate destinations in mind, but the tall trees and
heavy undergrowth make it hard to determine where each of the routes might take
you.
name: On a Faltering Trail

desc:
Amid the tall trees and thick undergrowth of this verdant forest a game trail
has sprung into being. Countless hooves and tiny paws must have stepped this
way, for there is a distinct path among the trees, and upon it very little
vegetation clings to life. The predominant trees in this area are deciduous, so
no soft bed of needles marks the way. Rather the ground is covered with a thick
leaf litter. Hard by the tree trunks, where fewer feet fall, this dense humus
provides a rich environment where weeds and wildflowers can thrive. And in the
more shaded patches a number of mushrooms grow.
name: A Turning Trail through the Trees

desc:
Curving here from west to south, a high rock face crosses the land. Growing hard
by this geographical feature the woodland thrives, with tall firs and pines
almost choking out the older deciduous trees. A few small plum trees can be seen
here and there, and among the thick undergrowth is a scattering of rhubarb
plants.
name: A Vertical Escarpment

desc:
A dirty, little pond sits in the shelter of the trees. Garbage and debris floats
on its surface, making the water look rather murky, yet there is no odour of
foulness or decay. Tall trees loom above you to all sides, and a variety of
wildflowers, bushes and shrubs flourish here.
name: At a Dirty Little Pond

desc:
Young trees and stout shrubs grow all about you, reclaiming this land as part of
the forest. A gated wall once stood here, dividing the wild woodland from the
farmland beyond, but this has tumbled down completely. All that remains are
irregular humps of stones and small boulders, upon which a variety of small
plants and mosses have started to grow.
name: At the Edge of the Woods

desc:
Coarse grasses and thick tufts of heather grow all around. Here and there, a few
low shrubs and bushes can be seen, as the gorse and brambles of the woodland
spread out across the fields. Small saplings struggle valiantly to survive, but
many have been cropped low to the ground by the local fauna. Neverthless, with
the incursion of fast-growing species like pine, the woods will soon manage to
reclaim this land.
name: Beside the Spreading Forest

desc:
The ground dips and rolls gently in all directions. At some distance to the west
a large forest rises out of the landscape, but here the only vegetation is
coarse, thin grass, thick tufts of soft heather, and a scattering of stout
bushes and tiny wildflowers. Unsheltered by hills or trees, this area is one
which is bleak in aspect, yet it retains a certain austere beauty.
name: Upon an Open Plain

desc:
Weeds and scattered brushwood grow all about, but a closer inspection of the
area shows that this was once a crop field. Deep furrows cut across the land
from north to south, but whatever was once planted here has now been choked out
by the hardier wild plants.
name: Fallow Land

desc:
You have entered a crude and apparently recently made tunnel. It does not seem
that the workers cared too much for the quality of their labour, for the walls,
floor and ceiling are all quite uneven. Winding westward, the passageway enters
what appears to be a larger, natural cavern.
name: A Crude Tunnel

desc:
The ledge gets more and more dilapidated as you move along it, and to the south,
the rubble looks so unstable that it would seem that digging in it might not be
a wise idea. However, it is obvious that some recent work has taken place here,
with large sections of the western wall brutally ripped away, leaving piles of
boulders scattered about, and what looks like the start of a tunnel opening out
before you.
name: A Dilapidated Ledge

desc:
You find yourself within a fairly large cave. Its walls and ceiling look quite
natural, although in places they have apparently been worn rather smooth over
the years. The floor is marked by numerous small scratches and scuff-marks, and
a few old bones are scattered here and there. Toward the rear of the cave the
floor drops off rapidly, and it looks like there might be a small tunnel which
leads off in that direction. To the north, only a few small bushes block the way
out.
name: A Small Bear Cave

desc:
Travel on this little stairway is difficult. Assorted rocks have been thrown
together to make a rough stairway. The light of a torch to the south shows a
small cavern.
name: A Little, Rocky Stairway

desc:
The foul stench of something which has remained too long unburied fill the
entire tunnel. Somewhere to the east a small light can be seen, revealing a
little stairway which curves downward, out of sight. To the south of here
another tunnel opens out before you.
name: A Smelly Tunnel

desc:
As you enter this room, the smell of decaying flesh hits you in the face. You
see to the north and south what looks to be piles of dead bodies and stripped
carcasses.
name: A Very Smelly Cavern

desc:
This narrow stone walkway perches high on a rock wall, overlooking a vast, and
seemingly bottomless chasm. Westward the slender pathway continues, climbing
steadily as it does so, and to the north it descends through a small fissure,
entering an enclosed tunnel.
name: A Small Walkway

desc:
The narrow walkway stretches downward to the east, but to the south it curves
gently upward, where it reaches a broad fissure in the wall of the cavern. To
the north lies only the cold stone wall, upon which this pathway perches so
precariously.
name: A Small Walkway

desc:
This peculiar side passage seems to burrow indiscriminately through the thick
dirt itself. Only creatures of massive strength could attempt such a feat, but
the lack of any refinements indicates a lack for the finer points of
construction. The smell of filth and loam fills the air.
name: A Gutted Tunnel

desc:
Onwards, the tunnel continues, a gaping maw carved in the very ground of Arda.
Roots stick out from all directions, as well as the blind eyes of very large
earthworms. The thick rancid smell of troll pervades the tunnel, leaving no
doubts as to who carved this path.
name: A Gutted Tunnel

desc:
Pieces of dirt and rock filter down from the ceiling, covering your head or
helmet in its filth. This does nothing to assuage your fears about the stability
of this tunnel, yet it seems to have gotten a lot of use. Large, splayed
footprints lead both ways, and a rancid, metallic smell dominates your senses.
name: A Gutted Tunnel

desc:
Ringed on three sides by hills, this shady vale seems lusher and more wholesome
than surrounding environs. A multitude of evergreens thrive in the fertile soil
which also supports equally plentiful brush and smaller greenery.
name: Woodlands below the Hills

desc:
A sheer bluff dominates the north section of this area, even a skilled
mountaineer could not ascend the steep limestone cliff. Pines, spruce, and birch
cluster below the cliffside in round bundles, relieved occasionaly by patches of
grass and heather. The dusty strip of the road can be spotted to the south,
while the woods continue below the uplands.
name: A Wooded Bluff

desc:
Bushy bundles of heather and briar cover the lightly sloping ground,
interspersed with solitary pines or larches. The flora's dry brown semblance
complements the dusky dun-coloured turf--even the trees here are dull and
dessicated. The winding ribbon of the road lies to your southeast, in the
opposite direction the land rears up into randomly rolling hills and cliffs.
name: Brush and Conifers

desc:
The walls of this cave are blackened by soot from many fires and covered by
crude carvings and dried blood. Bone splinters and small patches of cloth lie on
the hard earthen floor, along with wads of partially digested hair and a broken
wagon wheel. In the north-eastern corner of this cave stands a makeshift bed of
straw and pieces of cloth.
name: Sinister-looking Cave

desc:
This small area looks like a valley within the valley. The ground dips into a
rocky bowl, overgrown by thorny brambles and patches of moss. To the north an
opening in the side of the huge hill can be seen, dark and sinister. All sounds
are muffled here, and no birds can be heard. A few sets of huge footprints are
set in the mossy ground, leading towards the stonedoor set in a rockface to the
west.
name: Dark Dip in the Valley

desc:
The grey-blue cavern ceiling arches high above you, supported by tremendous
limestone pillars dripping with moisture. A dank, smoky odour pervades the air,
mixed with pungent food smells. An indescribable collection of debris and
mishmash occupies one section of the cavern; it would seem the occupants to be a
decidedly coarse and slovenly group.
name: A High-Vaulted Chamber

desc:
The faintest illumination turns this large grotto into a spectacle of form and
colour. The walls shimmer and sparkle in light; strange fluted columns rise from
the cave floor far up towards the ceiling, coloured translucent peach,
strawberry, and lavender. Water drips slowly from the ceiling, enhancing and
multiplying the crystalline brilliance of the rock surfaces by coating the forms
with an aqueous luster. Small passages open up on either side of the cavern, and
small rivulets of water coalesce and run sparkling down to a wide opening to the
east. A delicate pathway, whether chiseled by water or humanoid hands, spirals
up into the darkness.
name: Glimmering Grotto

desc:
Hollowed from the hills by the slow but pertinacious action of water, this
passageway bends and dips haphazardly; at one point wide and easily traversed,
at another requiring large travellers to squeeze through small gaps. Water drips
from the calciferous roof, redepositing its dissolved compounds into complicated
stalactite and stalagmite formations.
name: A Limestone Passage

desc:
This passage winds curvaceously downward into the heart of the uplands,
sometimes wide enough to facilitate easy passage, alternately narrowing so as to
require stooping or squeezing through small apertures. The walls are smooth
white limestone, finely grained with moist protusions from floor and
ceiling--water drips slowly from the ceiling into small puddles, echoing softly
up and down the tunnel.
name: A Limestone Passage

desc:
A mighty vein of quartz intrudes into the native limestone, casting glistening
white sparkles dancing across the cavern in the presence of light. The ceiling
is lower here than in the adjoining caverns, one must be wary lest they knock
their head upon a protruding stalactite. The faintest of echos mutters of
running water somewhere close yet far away.
name: Crystalline Cavern

desc:
Inky black and icy cold, the water seeping from the uplands has collected here,
trapped and cradled by some impermeable layer sandwiched inside the limestone. A
strange fusty odour pervades the grotto, and the lake seems to ripple randomly
in a fashion indicative of some animal life.
name: Subterranean Lake

desc:
This limestone cave, certainly inhabited at some juncture, now lies disused,
abandoned to the elements. In one corner lies a bed-frame of strong oak branches
beside some crumbling stools and chests. Near the door is a hearth with a red
plaster mantle marked with the fingerprints of its maker. A light ringing noise
draws you to the back of the cave--under a mossy chink in the rock is a little
pool brimming with clear water, fed by a spring that erupts from a furrow in the
wall.
name: An Abandoned Sheepfold

desc:
The numbing stench of dead and rotting carcases permeates the air in this
cramped, and sloping tunnel. Bending from north to west, the passageway makes a
precipitous descent. Northward it opens out somewhat, entering quite a large
cave.
name: A Long, Dark Tunnel

desc:
A light breeze blows up from a large crevice on the entire western side of this
narrow ledge. The little outcropping seems a little unstable as you walk across
it. You can't even imagine how far you would fall if this section of rock were
to give way to your weight.
name: A Narrow Ledge

desc:
You can go no further along the ledge, for here it comes to an abrupt end at a
massive rockfall. Even the floor underfoot appears to be unstable, and chilly
water seeps through the roof, and through broad cracks in the walls. It would
seem wise to return the way you came, as soon as possible.
name: Dead End

desc:
Wooded hills roll the landscape, their tops bald silhouettes against the
skyline. The hillsides are mainly covered by moss and young pines and spruce. To
the west a magnificent, sheer rockface flanks a very tall hill. A cliff to the
north obstructs passage in that direction. To the east the range of wooded hills
continues and south of here the Old East Road can be seen, like a brown ribbon
in the landscape.
name: Bald Hills

desc:
The Road runs alongside a hawthorn hedge, curving in unison with it from north
to west. The hedge is dense and bristling with small sharp thorns, but would
appear to have grown naturally as opposed to have been cultivated.
name: Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
Here the forest floor above forms a natural roof as a small stream flows in from
the south beneath the vines and flows north, deeper underground. The dirt is
packed with roots affording strength yet effectively blocking out most natural
light. To the south, tangled vines drape an opening like a fabric veil. There is
just enough solid ground next to the stream to walk safely without slipping.
name: Stream under an Overhang

desc:
From the south, a thin stream flows in to create a small underground lake. On
the edge of the lake, a well-crafted landing has been carved out of dark wood
with hand-worked walls and ceiling. The landing has been designed to dock a
handful of smaller boats. In the east wall, an iron-reinforced door looks
daunting as it blocks a tunnel opening.
name: Landing

desc:
Dense, packed dirt provides solid footing on a floor littered with crushed
bottles, crates, and other junk. A lit brass lantern is mounted securely on
thick beams of dark wood embedded into the walls. A narrow pipe runs upwards
through the natural roof, providing fresh air into the tunnel. The burrow
expands further to the north and east, whereas a solid-looking door blocks a
doorway to the west. Rows of boxes and shelves line the southern wall.
name: Smugglers' Burrow

desc:
This space is irregularly-shaped like most naturally-formed caverns. However,
the smooth walls and floor betray the work of skilled hands. Hairy roots poke
through the ceiling from above indicating trees or other vegetation overhead.
Several wooden crates and barrels line the eastern and southern walls.
name: Smugglers' Burrow

desc:
Mouldy planks form rows of simple shelves along the walls of this confined
depository. Sturdy wooden beams, installed to keep the ceiling from collapsing,
anchor the planks upright. There is a small air shaft covered in cobwebs between
the beams. A fairly flat, well-made stone floor is covered with a thick layer of
dust. Some shelves have rotted and fallen down, spilling their contents
carelessly.
name: Depository

desc:
Narrow paths run between rows of barrels, chests, and crates that seem to be
ducking under a very low ceiling that is criss-crossed by beams of wood
installed to prevent the earth above from collapsing. Above, a one foot wide
round shaft rises through the soil providing natural light and fresh air to the
burrow. Large, flat stones tile the floor up to a sturdy door in the north wall.
Through a small opening to the west, a tiny earth cave is visible. A large
storage chamber can be seen through the eastern wall.
name: Smugglers' Burrow

desc:
Dark, moist earth forms the walls, floor and ceiling of this passageway. It
travels east where it seems to open up into a larger cave. Plenty of footprints
can be spotted in the wet earth, tracking between the tunnel opening in the east
and up to a small pile of boulders high along the northern wall.
name: Escape Route

desc:
This small room might be an office; however, it is in disarray. A table of dark
wood and several crude chairs are toppled, broken and scattered about in the
room. A lantern, mounted high on a peg in the eastern wall, flickers with faint
light.
name: Littered Office

desc:
This tight space is a tiny chamber cut out from hard, rocky soil. Small niches
dug into the northern, eastern, and southern walls look as if they were intended
to hold items of value though most are empty now. The western wall is made of
heavy wooden planks covered in putrid fungi and mould.
name: Small Nook

desc:
A rounded tunnel slopes steeply into the dark inside of the hill. A layer of
smooth rubble covers the floor, making it impossible to stand up. The only way
of getting out of here is to continue sliding downwards into the dark deeps.
name: Very Steep Passage
